title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
summary: Kobie Crawford presents an approach to training smaller models with reinforcement learning to improve tool use and self-correction capabilities. A 4B model fine-tuned with RL to use the get_table_name function outperformed the much larger Qwen 3 235B on a database query task, achieving self-correction and accurate results at under $500 training cost.
writeup: Kobie Crawford of Snorkel argues for improving small model behavior through reinforcement learning rather than scaling parameter counts. The talk centers on fine-tuning a 4B model with RL to use a get_table_name function on a database query task, where it learned self-correction and outperformed the much larger Qwen 3 235B. The case for the approach rests on cost: the training ran at under $500 while still producing accurate results.
